name: Riotinto Mining Basin Loop
name_original: Berrocal desde Nerva — circular por Minas de Riotinto
description: This difficult 32.8-mile touring cycling route explores the unique mining landscapes and vibrant Río Tinto in the Berrocal from Nerva area.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Nerva traverse a landscape shaped by historical mining activity, featuring distinctive reddish terrain and reclaimed railway lines. The region is characterized by rolling hills and open countryside, providing varied gradients for cyclists. Notable features include the unique geological formations around the Río Tinto and the accessible Vías Verdes, offering paths away from traffic. This area in Andalusia, Spain, combines natural beauty with a rich industrial heritage. Best touring cycling routes around Nerva The most popular touring cycling route is Minas y Nacimiento del Río Tinto — circular desde Nerva, a 24.7 miles (39.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 32 minutes to complete, exploring the unique, mineral-rich landscapes of the Río Tinto area. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Riotinto Mining Basin Loop, a difficult 32.8 miles (52.7 km) path. This route offers expansive views of the distinctive reddish terrain shaped by historical mining. Local touring cyclists also love the Embalse de Gossán y Minas de Riotinto — circular desde Nerva, a 21.5 miles (34.6 km) trail leading through the historic Minas de Riotinto and past the Embalse de Gossán, often completed in about 2 hours 33 minutes. Touring cycling around Nerva is defined by historical mining landscapes, reddish riverbeds, and reclaimed railway lines. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes are rated as difficult. The routes in Nerva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from 1 review. More than 80 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Nerva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Nerva?
A: There are 11 touring cycling routes around Nerva available on komoot. These routes offer a variety of experiences, primarily focusing on the region's unique mining landscapes and natural features.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on touring cycling routes in Nerva?
A: Touring cycling routes around Nerva traverse a landscape shaped by historical mining activity, featuring distinctive reddish terrain and reclaimed railway lines. You'll encounter rolling hills, open countryside, and unique geological formations around the Río Tinto, often described as Martian-like due to mineral deposits. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366191055/minas-y-nacimiento-del-rio-tinto-circular-desde-nerva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Minas y Nacimiento del Río Tinto — circular desde Nerva&lt;/a&gt; route is a great example of this unique scenery.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes for beginners in Nerva?
A: The touring cycling routes around Nerva are generally geared towards more experienced cyclists. Out of the 11 routes, 8 are rated as difficult and 3 as moderate. There are currently no routes specifically classified as easy, so beginners should be prepared for challenging terrain and elevation changes.

Q: What are some notable attractions or landmarks I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The Nerva area is rich in historical mining sites and natural monuments. Along the routes, you can explore the profound impact of historical mining at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Minas de Riotinto&lt;/a&gt;, witness the unique coloration of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4402997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Río Tinto&lt;/a&gt;, or see the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5287308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corta Atalaya Mine&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366240481/embalse-de-gossan-y-minas-de-riotinto-circular-desde-nerva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Gossán y Minas de Riotinto — circular desde Nerva&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6268303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gossán Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Nerva?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Nerva are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366191055/minas-y-nacimiento-del-rio-tinto-circular-desde-nerva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Minas y Nacimiento del Río Tinto — circular desde Nerva&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1365755959/riotinto-mining-basin-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riotinto Mining Basin Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366234493/nerva-campofrio-and-la-granada-de-rio-tinto-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nerva, Campofrío, and La Granada de Río-Tinto Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Nerva?
A: Nerva, located in Andalusia, Spain, generally offers favorable conditions for touring cycling for much of the year. Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) are often ideal, providing pleasant temperatures and less intense sun than the summer months. Winters are typically mild, making it possible to cycle, though some days may be cooler or wetter.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Nerva?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on cycling routes can vary, many outdoor areas in Spain are dog-friendly, especially on natural trails. It's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially near roads or in areas with wildlife, and to carry water for both yourself and your pet. Check local signage for any specific restrictions on particular routes or protected areas.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or amenities along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes around Nerva often traverse natural and historical mining landscapes. While specific cafes or pubs directly on the trails are not detailed in the route information, Nerva and surrounding towns like Minas de Riotinto will offer amenities. It's recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31513210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Río Tinto loop from Nerva&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Nerva?
A: The touring cycling routes in Nerva are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the unique, mineral-rich landscapes, the historical mining sites, and the opportunity to explore reclaimed railway lines like the Vías Verdes, which offer scenic paths away from traffic.

Q: How long do touring cycling routes around Nerva typically take to complete?
A: The duration of touring cycling routes around Nerva varies depending on the distance and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366191055/minas-y-nacimiento-del-rio-tinto-circular-desde-nerva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Minas y Nacimiento del Río Tinto — circular desde Nerva&lt;/a&gt;, a 39.7 km route, typically takes around 3 hours 32 minutes. Longer routes, such as the 66.8 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366234493/nerva-campofrio-and-la-granada-de-rio-tinto-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nerva, Campofrío, and La Granada de Río-Tinto Loop&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 4 hours.

Q: Is public transport available to access touring cycling routes in Nerva?
A: Nerva is a municipality in Andalusia, Spain, and while local bus services connect it to nearby towns, specific public transport options designed for cyclists with bikes directly to trailheads are not detailed. It's generally advisable to plan your route to start and end in Nerva or a nearby town with parking facilities, or to arrange private transport if you're bringing your own bike.
tips: Tour between the facilities of the old iron and copper mine - historically the municipality has been closely linked to mining activity.

It is one of the several buildings of the Riotinto Museum that is distributed in several of the old mines within what is known as the Riotinto Mining Park. More information can be obtained on the official website https://parquemineroderiotinto.es/
Obligatory stopping point if you do the road. There are incredible views even of the Rio Tinto mining railway.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging 52.7 km circular route is classified as a 'difficult' touring bicycle ride. It typically takes around 4 hours to complete, but this can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the unique scenery.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and described as a 'Hard bike ride' requiring 'very good fitness'. With a significant elevation gain of 922 meters and varied terrain including unpaved sections and singletrack, it is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and can be started from Nerva or the Berrocal area. While specific parking details aren't provided, Nerva is a good starting point. Look for parking options within the town or near the trailhead access points.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is mixed and varied. You'll encounter 22.9 km of asphalt, 19.6 km of paved roads, but also 10.2 km of unpaved sections and 9.54 km of singletrack. A segment from Berrocal to Nerva along the Río Tinto follows an old railway line, which involves frequently riding on ballast (stones) for about three kilometers. Cyclists may need to push their bikes on some difficult segments.

faqs.question: What unique scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: This route offers a truly unique 'mineral universe'. You'll cycle through the heart of the Minas de Riotinto mining basin, witnessing a landscape with chromatic scales of red, yellow, green, and black. A major highlight is the Río Tinto itself, famous for its intense red and yellow hues due to high iron content. This environment has been compared to a lunar landscape and is of scientific interest.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several fascinating points of interest. These include the historic Minas de Riotinto, the Riotinto Mining Museum, and the panoramic El Berrocal Viewpoint. The trail also follows sections of old mining railways, showcasing historical mining facilities, mineral washing areas, and workshops.

faqs.question: Does this route follow any official or well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several official trails. Notably, it covers significant portions of the Vía Verde de Riotinto (approximately 46% of the route), and also intersects with the Camino de Santiago del Sur (15%) and the Vía Verde del Odiel (6%).

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify whether dogs are allowed on this particular cycling route or within the Río Tinto Protected Landscape. It's advisable to check local regulations or contact the park authorities directly before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this area?
faqs.answer: This trail is located within the Río Tinto Protected Landscape. While the trail itself may not require a specific permit, some attractions like the Riotinto Mining Museum may have entrance fees. It's recommended to check for any specific regulations or fees related to the protected landscape or its facilities before your visit.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the mixed terrain, including over 10 km of unpaved sections, nearly 10 km of singletrack, and segments with ballast, a touring bike with wider tires, good suspension, or a more rugged design is highly recommended. Traditional road touring bicycles may struggle on some segments.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is typically milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the extreme heat of summer and potential winter rains.
